instruction: You are the QEOS Requirements Agent. Given business requirements, generate test scenarios, test cases, risk analysis, and coverage matrix as JSON.
input:
Source type: user_story

Requirements:
Feature: OrangeHRM Login, Navigation, and Logout

Background:
Given the user opens "https://opensource-demo.orangehrmlive.com/web/index.php/auth/login"
And the OrangeHRM login page is displayed

@smoke @login
Scenario: Login successfully with valid credentials
When the user enters username "Admin"
And the user enters password "admin123"
And the user clicks the "Login" button
Then the Dashboard page should be displayed
And the left navigation menu should be visible

@regression @navigation
Scenario Outline: Navigate to each left navigation menu item
Given the user is logged into OrangeHRM
When the user clicks the "<Menu>" menu item from the left navigation
Then the "<Menu>" page should be displayed
And the page header should contain "<Menu>"

```
Examples:
  | Menu          |
  | Admin         |
  | PIM           |
  | Leave         |
  | Time          |
  | Recruitment   |
  | My Info       |
  | Performance   |
  | Dashboard     |
  | Directory     |
  | Maintenance   |
  | Claim         |
  | Buzz          |
```

@e2e
Scenario: Login, navigate all menus, and logout
Given the user is on the OrangeHRM login page
When the user enters username "Admin"
And the user enters password "admin123"
And the user clicks the "Login" button
Then the Dashboard page should be displayed

```
When the user clicks the "Admin" menu
Then the "Admin" page should be displayed

When the user clicks the "PIM" menu
Then the "PIM" page should be displayed

When the user clicks the "Leave" menu
Then the "Leave" page should be displayed

When the user clicks the "Time" menu
Then the "Time" page should be displayed

When the user clicks the "Recruitment" menu
Then the "Recruitment" page should be displayed

When the user clicks the "My Info" menu
Then the "My Info" page should be displayed

When the user clicks the "Performance" menu
Then the "Performance" page should be displayed

When the user clicks the "Dashboard" menu
Then the Dashboard page should be displayed

When the user clicks the "Directory" menu
Then the "Directory" page should be displayed

When the user clicks the "Maintenance" menu
Then the "Maintenance" page should be displayed

When the user clicks the "Claim" menu
Then the "Claim" page should be displayed

When the user clicks the "Buzz" menu
Then the "Buzz" page should be displayed

When the user clicks the user profile menu in the top-right corner
And the user selects "Logout"
Then the Login page should be displayed
And the username field should be visible
```

Source type: user_story

Requirements:
# Playwright Automation Script Generation Prompt

Create a production-grade Playwright automation project using TypeScript for the OrangeHRM Demo application.

Application URL:
https://opensource-demo.orangehrmlive.com/web/index.php/auth/login

## Objective

Build an end-to-end automation suite that:

1. Launches browser.
2. Navigates to OrangeHRM login page.
3. Logs in using valid credentials.
4. Navigates through every available left navigation menu.
5. Verifies successful page load for each menu.
6. Captures screenshots for every page.
7. Handles dynamic loading and synchronization.
8. Logs execution details.
9. Logs out successfully.
10. Generates Playwright HTML reports.

## Test Data

Username: Admin
Password: admin123

## Framework Requirements

### Technology Stack

* Playwright
* TypeScript
* Page Object Model (POM)
* Playwright Test Runner

### Folder Structure

src/
├── pages/
│   ├── LoginPage.ts
│   ├── DashboardPage.ts
│   ├── NavigationPage.ts
│   └── LogoutPage.ts
├── tests/
│   └── orangehrm-navigation.spec.ts
├── utils/
│   ├── logger.ts
│   └── screenshotHelper.ts
├── fixtures/
│   └── testData.ts
└── playwright.config.ts

## Functional Flow

### Step 1: Login

* Open login page.
* Verify login form displayed.
* Enter username and password.
* Click Login.
* Verify Dashboard page loaded.

### Step 2: Navigate Every Left Menu

Dynamically identify and click all visible menu items from the left navigation panel.

Expected menus include:

* Admin
* PIM
* Leave
* Time
* Recruitment
* My Info
* Performance
* Dashboard
* Directory
* Maintenance
* Claim
* Buzz

For each menu:

* Click menu item.
* Wait for page load.
* Verify page title/header exists.
* Verify URL changes appropriately.
* Capture screenshot.
* Log navigation result.

### Step 3: Submenu Validation

If a menu contains expandable submenus:

* Expand submenu.
* Navigate to each submenu.
* Verify page loads successfully.
* Capture screenshot.
* Return to parent menu.

### Step 4: Assertions

For every page:

* Verify page header is visible.
* Verify URL contains expected path.
* Verify no error message is displayed.
* Verify page content area is loaded.

### Step 5: Error Handling

Implement:

* Retry mechanism.
* Screenshot on failure.
* Detailed error logging.
* Graceful test continuation where possible.

### Step 6: Logout

* Click user profile icon.
* Click Logout.
* Verify redirected to login page.
* Verify login form displayed.

## Reporting

Generate:

* Playwright HTML Report
* Execution screenshots
* Trace file on failure
* Video recording on failure

## Coding Standards

* Use async/await.
* Avoid hardcoded waits.
* Use robust locators.
* Prefer getByRole(), getByText(), and data-driven locators.
* Follow Page Object Model principles.
* Add reusable utility methods.

## Advanced Requirement

Create a generic navigation method:

navigateToAllMenus()

This method should:

* Read all menu items dynamically from the left panel.
* Iterate through them.
* Click each menu.
* Validate page load.
* Capture evidence.
* Record pass/fail status.

Return a navigation summary object:

{
totalMenus: number,
passedMenus: number,
failedMenus: number,
failedPages: string[]
}

## Deliverables

Generate complete code for:

1. playwright.config.ts
2. LoginPage.ts
3. DashboardPage.ts
4. NavigationPage.ts
5. LogoutPage.ts
6. orangehrm-navigation.spec.ts
7. Utility classes
8. Sample execution report configuration
9. README with execution instructions

The generated code should be executable without requiring major modifications.
